NKJV.1: And seeing the multitudes, He went up on a mountain, and when He was seated His disciples came to Him.
NKJV.2: Then He opened His mouth and taught them, saying:
NKJV.3: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, For theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.4: Blessed are those who mourn, For they shall be comforted.
NKJV.5: Blessed are the meek, For they shall inherit the earth.
NKJV.6: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, For they shall be filled.
NKJV.7: Blessed are the merciful, For they shall obtain mercy.
NKJV.8: Blessed are the pure in heart, For they shall see God.
NKJV.9: Blessed are the peacemakers, For they shall be called sons of God.
NKJV.10: Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness’ sake, For theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.11: “Blessed are you when they revile and persecute you, and say all kinds of  evil against you falsely for My sake.
NKJV.12: Rejoice and be exceedingly glad, for great is your reward in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
NKJV.13: “You are the salt of the earth;  but if the salt loses its flavor, how shall it be seasoned? It is then good for nothing but to be thrown out and trampled underfoot by men.
NKJV.14: “You are the light of the world. A city that is set on a hill cannot be hidden.
NKJV.15: Nor do they light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a lampstand, and it gives light to all who are in the house.
NKJV.16: Let your light so shine before men,  that they may see your good works and  glorify your Father in heaven.
NKJV.17: “Do not think that I came to destroy the Law or the Prophets. I did not come to destroy but to fulfill.
NKJV.18: For assuredly, I say to you,  till heaven and earth pass away, one jot or one tittle will by no means pass from the law till all is fulfilled.
NKJV.19: Whoever therefore breaks one of the least of these commandments, and teaches men so, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever does and teaches them, he shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.20: For I say to you, that unless your righteousness exceeds the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, you will by no means enter the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.21: “You have heard that it was said to those of old,  ‘You shall not murder, and whoever murders will be in danger of the judgment.’
NKJV.22: But I say to you that  whoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment. And whoever says to his brother,  ‘Raca!’ shall be in danger of the council. But whoever says, ‘You fool!’ shall be in danger of hell fire.
NKJV.23: Therefore  if you bring your gift to the altar, and there remember that your brother has something against you,
NKJV.24: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
NKJV.25: Agree with your adversary quickly,  while you are on the way with him, lest your adversary deliver you to the judge, the judge hand you over to the officer, and you be thrown into prison.
NKJV.26: Assuredly, I say to you, you will by no means get out of there till you have paid the last penny.
NKJV.27: “You have heard that it was said to those of old, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
NKJV.28: But I say to you that whoever  looks at a woman to lust for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NKJV.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, pluck it out and cast it from you; for it is more profitable for you that one of your members perish, than for your whole body to be cast into hell.
NKJV.30: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and cast it from you; for it is more profitable for you that one of your members perish, than for your whole body to be cast into hell.
NKJV.31: “Furthermore it has been said,  ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
NKJV.32: But I say to you that  whoever divorces his wife for any reason except sexual immorality causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a woman who is divorced commits adultery.
NKJV.33: “Again you have heard that  it was said to those of old,  ‘You shall not swear falsely, but  shall perform your oaths to the Lord.’
NKJV.34: But I say to you,  do not swear at all: neither by heaven, for it is  God’s throne;
NKJV.35: nor by the earth, for it is His footstool; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of  the great King.
NKJV.36: Nor shall you swear by your head, because you cannot make one hair white or black.
NKJV.37: But let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes,’ and your ‘No,’ ‘No.’ For whatever is more than these is from the evil one.
NKJV.38: “You have heard that it was said,  ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
NKJV.39: But I tell you not to resist an evil person.  But whoever slaps you on your right cheek, turn the other to him also.
NKJV.40: If anyone wants to sue you and take away your tunic, let him have your cloak also.
NKJV.41: And whoever  compels you to go one mile, go with him two.
NKJV.42: Give to him who asks you, and  from him who wants to borrow from you do not turn away.
NKJV.43: “You have heard that it was said,  ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
NKJV.44: But I say to you,  love your enemies, bless those who curse you,  do good to those who hate you, and pray  for those who spitefully use you and persecute you,
NKJV.45: that you may be sons of your Father in heaven; for  He makes His sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the just and on the unjust.
NKJV.46: For if you love those who love you, what reward have you? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
NKJV.47: And if you greet your brethren only, what do you do more than others? Do not even the tax collectors do so?
NKJV.48: Therefore you shall be perfect, just  as your Father in heaven is perfect.

ESV.1: Seeing the crowds, he went up on the mountain, and when he sat down, his disciples came to him.
ESV.2: And he opened his mouth and taught them, saying:
ESV.3: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
ESV.4: “Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
ESV.5: “Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
ESV.6: “Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
ESV.7: “Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy.
ESV.8: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
ESV.9: “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons  of God.
ESV.10: “Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
ESV.11: “Blessed are you when others revile you and persecute you and utter all kinds of evil against you falsely on my account.
ESV.12: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
ESV.13: “You are the salt of the earth, but if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled under people's feet.
ESV.14: “You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.
ESV.15: Nor do people light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a stand, and it gives light to all in the house.
ESV.16: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that  they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.
ESV.17: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.
ESV.18: For truly, I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not an iota, not a dot, will pass from the Law until all is accomplished.
ESV.19: Therefore whoever relaxes one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
ESV.20: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
ESV.21: “You have heard that it was said to those of old, ‘You shall not murder; and whoever murders will be liable to judgment.’
ESV.22: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother  will be liable to judgment; whoever insults  his brother will be liable to the council; and whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be liable to the hell  of fire.
ESV.23: So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
ESV.24: leave your gift there before the altar and go. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
ESV.25: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are going with him to court, lest your accuser hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you be put in prison.
ESV.26: Truly, I say to you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
ESV.27: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
ESV.28: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lustful intent has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
ESV.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
ESV.30: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of your members than that your whole body go into hell.
ESV.31: “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
ESV.32: But I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of sexual immorality, makes her commit adultery, and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
ESV.33: “Again you have heard that it was said to those of old, ‘You shall not swear falsely, but shall perform to the Lord what you have sworn.’
ESV.34: But I say to you, Do not take an oath at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
ESV.35: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
ESV.36: And do not take an oath by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
ESV.37: Let what you say be simply ‘Yes’ or ‘No’; anything more than this comes from evil.
ESV.38: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
ESV.39: But I say to you, Do not resist the one who is evil. But if anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.
ESV.40: And if anyone would sue you and take your tunic,  let him have your cloak as well.
ESV.41: And if anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
ESV.42: Give to the one who begs from you, and do not refuse the one who would borrow from you.
ESV.43: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
ESV.44: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
ESV.45: so that you may be sons of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the just and on the unjust.
ESV.46: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
ESV.47: And if you greet only your brothers,  what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
ESV.48: You therefore must be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

NASB.1: When Jesus saw the crowds, He went up on the mountain; and after He sat down, His disciples came to Him.
NASB.2: He opened His mouth and [began] to teach them, saying,
NASB.3: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NASB.4: 'Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
NASB.5: 'Blessed are the gentle, for they shall inherit the earth.
NASB.6: 'Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
NASB.7: 'Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy.
NASB.8: 'Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
NASB.9: 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.
NASB.10: 'Blessed are those who have been persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NASB.11: 'Blessed are you when [people] insult you and persecute you, and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of Me.
NASB.12: 'Rejoice and be glad, for your reward in heaven is great; for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
NASB.13: 'You are the salt of the earth; but if the salt has become tasteless, how can it be made salty [again]? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled under foot by men.
NASB.14: 'You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden;
NASB.15: nor does [anyone] light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on the lampstand, and it gives light to all who are in the house.
NASB.16: 'Let your light shine before men in such a way that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
NASB.17: 'Do not think that I came to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I did not come to abolish but to fulfill.
NASB.18: 'For truly I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not the smallest letter or stroke shall pass from the Law until all is accomplished.
NASB.19: 'Whoever then annuls one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others [to do] the same, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever keeps and teaches [them], he shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
NASB.20: 'For I say to you that unless your righteousness surpasses [that] of the scribes and Pharisees, you will not enter the kingdom of heaven.
NASB.21: 'You have heard that the ancients were told, 'YOU SHALL NOT COMMIT MURDER ' and 'Whoever commits murder shall be liable to the court.'
NASB.22: 'But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother shall be guilty before the court; and whoever says to his brother, 'You good-for-nothing,' shall be guilty before the supreme court; and whoever says, 'You fool,' shall be guilty [enough to go] into the fiery hell.
NASB.23: 'Therefore if you are presenting your offering at the altar, and there remember that your brother has something against you,
NASB.24: leave your offering there before the altar and go; first be reconciled to your brother, and then come and present your offering.
NASB.25: 'Make friends quickly with your opponent at law while you are with him on the way, so that your opponent may not hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the officer, and you be thrown into prison.
NASB.26: 'Truly I say to you, you will not come out of there until you have paid up the last cent.
NASB.27: 'You have heard that it was said, 'YOU SHALL NOT COMMIT ADULTERY';
NASB.28: but I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lust for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NASB.29: 'If your right eye makes you stumble, tear it out and throw it from you; for it is better for you to lose one of the parts of your body, than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
NASB.30: 'If your right hand makes you stumble, cut it off and throw it from you; for it is better for you to lose one of the parts of your body, than for your whole body to go into hell.
NASB.31: 'It was said, 'WHOEVER SENDS HIS WIFE AWAY, LET HIM GIVE HER A CERTIFICATE OF DIVORCE';
NASB.32: but I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except for [the] reason of unchastity, makes her commit adultery; and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
NASB.33: 'Again, you have heard that the ancients were told, 'YOU SHALL NOT MAKE FALSE VOWS, BUT SHALL FULFILL YOUR VOWS TO THE LORD.'
NASB.34: 'But I say to you, make no oath at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
NASB.35: or by the earth, for it is the footstool of His feet, or by Jerusalem, for it is THE CITY OF THE GREAT KING.
NASB.36: 'Nor shall you make an oath by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
NASB.37: 'But let your statement be, 'Yes, yes ' [or] 'No, no'; anything beyond these is of evil.
NASB.38: 'You have heard that it was said, 'AN EYE FOR AN EYE, AND A TOOTH FOR A TOOTH.'
NASB.39: 'But I say to you, do not resist an evil person; but whoever slaps you on your right cheek, turn the other to him also.
NASB.40: 'If anyone wants to sue you and take your shirt, let him have your coat also.
NASB.41: 'Whoever forces you to go one mile, go with him two.
NASB.42: 'Give to him who asks of you, and do not turn away from him who wants to borrow from you.
NASB.43: 'You have heard that it was said, 'YOU SHALL LOVE YOUR NEIGHBOR and hate your enemy.'
NASB.44: 'But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
NASB.45: so that you may be sons of your Father who is in heaven; for He causes His sun to rise on [the] evil and [the] good, and sends rain on [the] righteous and [the] unrighteous.
NASB.46: 'For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
NASB.47: 'If you greet only your brothers, what more are you doing [than others]? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
NASB.48: 'Therefore you are to be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

NLT.1: One day as he saw the crowds gathering, Jesus went up on the mountainside and sat down. His disciples gathered around him,
NLT.2: and he began to teach them.
NLT.3:
“God blesses those who are poor and realize their need for him,
for the Kingdom of Heaven is theirs.
NLT.4:
God blesses those who mourn,
for they will be comforted.
NLT.5:
God blesses those who are humble,
for they will inherit the whole earth.
NLT.6:
God blesses those who hunger and thirst for justice,
for they will be satisfied.
NLT.7:
God blesses those who are merciful,
for they will be shown mercy.
NLT.8:
God blesses those whose hearts are pure,
for they will see God.
NLT.9:
God blesses those who work for peace,
for they will be called the children of God.
NLT.10:
God blesses those who are persecuted for doing right,
for the Kingdom of Heaven is theirs.
NLT.11: “God blesses you when people mock you and persecute you and lie about you and say all sorts of evil things against you because you are my followers.
NLT.12: Be happy about it! Be very glad! For a great reward awaits you in heaven. And remember, the ancient prophets were persecuted in the same way.
NLT.13: “You are the salt of the earth. But what good is salt if it has lost its flavor? Can you make it salty again? It will be thrown out and trampled underfoot as worthless.
NLT.14: “You are the light of the world — like a city on a hilltop that cannot be hidden.
NLT.15: No one lights a lamp and then puts it under a basket. Instead, a lamp is placed on a stand, where it gives light to everyone in the house.
NLT.16: In the same way, let your good deeds shine out for all to see, so that everyone will praise your heavenly Father.
NLT.17: “Don’t misunderstand why I have come. I did not come to abolish the law of Moses or the writings of the prophets. No, I came to accomplish their purpose.
NLT.18: I tell you the truth, until heaven and earth disappear, not even the smallest detail of God’s law will disappear until its purpose is achieved.
NLT.19: So if you ignore the least commandment and teach others to do the same, you will be called the least in the Kingdom of Heaven. But anyone who obeys God’s laws and teaches them will be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven.
NLT.20: “But I warn you — unless your righteousness is better than the righteousness of the teachers of religious law and the Pharisees, you will never enter the Kingdom of Heaven!
NLT.21: “You have heard that our ancestors were told, ‘You must not murder. If you commit murder, you are subject to judgment.’
NLT.22: But I say, if you are even angry with someone, you are subject to judgment! If you call someone an idiot, you are in danger of being brought before the court. And if you curse someone, you are in danger of the fires of hell.
NLT.23: “So if you are presenting a sacrifice at the altar in the Temple and you suddenly remember that someone has something against you,
NLT.24: leave your sacrifice there at the altar. Go and be reconciled to that person. Then come and offer your sacrifice to God.
NLT.25: “When you are on the way to court with your adversary, settle your differences quickly. Otherwise, your accuser may hand you over to the judge, who will hand you over to an officer, and you will be thrown into prison.
NLT.26: And if that happens, you surely won’t be free again until you have paid the last penny.
NLT.27: “You have heard the commandment that says, ‘You must not commit adultery.’
NLT.28: But I say, anyone who even looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NLT.29: So if your eye — even your good eye — causes you to lust, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
NLT.30: And if your hand — even your stronger hand — causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
NLT.31: “You have heard the law that says, ‘A man can divorce his wife by merely giving her a written notice of divorce.’
NLT.32: But I say that a man who divorces his wife, unless she has been unfaithful, causes her to commit adultery. And anyone who marries a divorced woman also commits adultery.
NLT.33: “You have also heard that our ancestors were told, ‘You must not break your vows; you must carry out the vows you make to the LORD.’
NLT.34: But I say, do not make any vows! Do not say, ‘By heaven!’ because heaven is God’s throne.
NLT.35: And do not say, ‘By the earth!’ because the earth is his footstool. And do not say, ‘By Jerusalem!’ for Jerusalem is the city of the great King.
NLT.36: Do not even say, ‘By my head!’ for you can’t turn one hair white or black.
NLT.37: Just say a simple, ‘Yes, I will,’ or ‘No, I won’t.’ Anything beyond this is from the evil one.
NLT.38: “You have heard the law that says the punishment must match the injury: ‘An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.’
NLT.39: But I say, do not resist an evil person! If someone slaps you on the right cheek, offer the other cheek also.
NLT.40: If you are sued in court and your shirt is taken from you, give your coat, too.
NLT.41: If a soldier demands that you carry his gear for a mile, carry it two miles.
NLT.42: Give to those who ask, and don’t turn away from those who want to borrow.
NLT.43: “You have heard the law that says, ‘Love your neighbor’ and hate your enemy.
NLT.44: But I say, love your enemies! Pray for those who persecute you!
NLT.45: In that way, you will be acting as true children of your Father in heaven. For he gives his sunlight to both the evil and the good, and he sends rain on the just and the unjust alike.
NLT.46: If you love only those who love you, what reward is there for that? Even corrupt tax collectors do that much.
NLT.47: If you are kind only to your friends, how are you different from anyone else? Even pagans do that.
NLT.48: But you are to be perfect, even as your Father in heaven is perfect.

WEB.1: Seeing the multitudes, he went up onto the mountain. When he had sat down, his disciples came to him.
WEB.2: He opened his mouth and taught them, saying,
WEB.3: “Blessed are the poor in spirit,  for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.
WEB.4: Blessed are those who mourn,  for they shall be comforted.
WEB.5: Blessed are the gentle,  for they shall inherit the earth.
WEB.6: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst after righteousness,   for they shall be filled.
WEB.7: Blessed are the merciful,  for they shall obtain mercy.
WEB.8: Blessed are the pure in heart,  for they shall see God.
WEB.9: Blessed are the peacemakers,  for they shall be called children of God.
WEB.10: Blessed are those who have been persecuted for righteousness’ sake,   for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.
WEB.11: “Blessed are you when people reproach you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
WEB.12: Rejoice, and be exceedingly glad, for great is your reward in heaven. For that is how they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
WEB.13: “You are the salt of the earth, but if the salt has lost its flavor, with what will it be salted? It is then good for nothing, but to be cast out and trodden under the feet of men.
WEB.14: You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can’t be hidden.
WEB.15: Neither do you light a lamp, and put it under a measuring basket, but on a stand; and it shines to all who are in the house.
WEB.16: Even so, let your light shine before men; that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
WEB.17: “Don’t think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn’t come to destroy, but to fulfill.
WEB.18: For most certainly, I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not even one smallest letter  or one tiny pen stroke  shall in any way pass away from the law, until all things are accomplished.
WEB.19: Whoever, therefore, shall break one of these least commandments, and teach others to do so, shall be called least in the Kingdom of Heaven; but whoever shall do and teach them shall be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven.
WEB.20: For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, there is no way you will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.
WEB.21: “You have heard that it was said to the ancient ones, ‘You shall not murder;’  and ‘Whoever murders will be in danger of the judgment.’
WEB.22: But I tell you, that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause   will be in danger of the judgment; and whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca!’   will be in danger of the council; and whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of Gehenna.
WEB.23: “If therefore you are offering your gift at the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you,
WEB.24: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
WEB.25: Agree with your adversary quickly, while you are with him on the way; lest perhaps the prosecutor deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and you be cast into prison.
WEB.26: Most certainly I tell you, you shall by no means get out of there, until you have paid the last penny.
WEB.27: “You have heard that it was said,   ‘You shall not commit adultery;’
WEB.28: but I tell you that everyone who gazes at a woman to lust after her has committed adultery with her already in his heart.
WEB.29: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.
WEB.30: If your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off, and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.
WEB.31: “It was also said, ‘Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,’
WEB.32: but I tell you that whoever puts away his wife, except for the cause of sexual immorality, makes her an adulteress; and whoever marries her when she is put away commits adultery.
WEB.33: “Again you have heard that it was said to them of old time, ‘You shall not make false vows, but shall perform to the Lord your vows,’
WEB.34: but I tell you, don’t swear at all: neither by heaven, for it is the throne of God;
WEB.35: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
WEB.36: Neither shall you swear by your head, for you can’t make one hair white or black.
WEB.37: But let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes’ and your ‘No’ be ‘No.’ Whatever is more than these is of the evil one.
WEB.38: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.’
WEB.39: But I tell you, don’t resist him who is evil; but whoever strikes you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also.
WEB.40: If anyone sues you to take away your coat, let him have your cloak also.
WEB.41: Whoever compels you to go one mile, go with him two.
WEB.42: Give to him who asks you, and don’t turn away him who desires to borrow from you.
WEB.43: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor   and hate your enemy.’
WEB.44: But I tell you, love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who mistreat you and persecute you,
WEB.45: that you may be children of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the just and the unjust.
WEB.46: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Don’t even the tax collectors do the same?
WEB.47: If you only greet your friends, what more do you do than others? Don’t even the tax collectors  do the same?
WEB.48: Therefore you shall be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.

Whoever insults <sup>[6]</sup> his brother or sister, will be subject to the court. <sup>[7]</sup> Whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be subject to hellfire. <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>

CSB17.27: “You have heard that it was said, Do not commit adultery. [11] ⓕ

CSB17.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, <sup>ⓗ</sup> gouge it out and throw it away. For it is better that you lose one of the parts of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell. <sup>ⓘ</sup>

AMP.1: When Jesus saw the crowds, He went up on the mountain; and when He was seated, His disciples came to Him.
AMP.2: Then He began to teach them, saying,
AMP.3: "Blessed [spiritually prosperous, happy, to be admired] are the poor in spirit [those devoid of spiritual arrogance, those who regard themselves as insignificant], for theirs is the kingdom of heaven [both now and forever].
AMP.4: "Blessed [forgiven, refreshed by God's grace] are those who mourn [over their sins and repent], for they will be comforted [when the burden of sin is lifted].
AMP.5: "Blessed [inwardly peaceful, spiritually secure, worthy of respect] are the gentle [the kind-hearted, the sweet-spirited, the self-controlled], for they will inherit the earth.
AMP.6: "Blessed [joyful, nourished by God's goodness] are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness [those who actively seek right standing with God], for they will be [completely] satisfied.
AMP.7: "Blessed [content, sheltered by God's promises] are the merciful, for they will receive mercy.
AMP.8: "Blessed [anticipating God's presence, spiritually mature] are the pure in heart [those with integrity, moral courage, and godly character], for they will see God.
AMP.9: "Blessed [spiritually calm with life-joy in God's favor] are the makers and maintainers of peace, for they will [express His character and] be called the sons of God.
AMP.10: "Blessed [comforted by inner peace and God's love] are those who are persecuted for doing that which is morally right, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven [both now and forever].
AMP.11: "Blessed [morally courageous and spiritually alive with life-joy in God's goodness] are you when people insult you and persecute you, and falsely say all kinds of evil things against you because of [your association with] Me.
AMP.12: Be glad and exceedingly joyful, for your reward in heaven is great [absolutely inexhaustible]; for in this same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
AMP.13: "You are the salt of the earth; but if the salt has lost its taste (purpose), how can it be made salty? It is no longer good for anything, but to be thrown out and walked on by people [when the walkways are wet and slippery].
AMP.14: "You are the light of [Christ to] the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden;
AMP.15: nor does anyone light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a lampstand, and it gives light to all who are in the house.
AMP.16: Let your light shine before men in such a way that they may see your good deeds and moral excellence, and [recognize and honor and] glorify your Father who is in heaven.
AMP.17: "Do not think that I came to do away with or undo the Law [of Moses] or the [writings of the] Prophets; I did not come to destroy but to fulfill.
AMP.18: For I assure you and most solemnly say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not the smallest letter or stroke [of the pen] will pass from the Law until all things [which it foreshadows] are accomplished.
AMP.19: So whoever breaks one of the least [important] of these commandments, and teaches others to do the same, will be called least [important] in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever practices and teaches them, he will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
AMP.20: "For I say to you that unless your righteousness (uprightness, moral essence) is more than that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
AMP.21: "You have heard that it was said to the men of old, '<e>You shall not murder</e>,' and 'Whoever murders shall be guilty before the court.'
AMP.22: But I say to you that everyone who continues to be angry with his brother or harbors malice against him shall be guilty before the court; and whoever speaks [contemptuously and insultingly] to his brother, Raca (You empty-headed idiot)!' shall be guilty before the supreme court (Sanhedrin); and whoever says, 'You fool!' shall be in danger of the fiery hell.
AMP.23: So if you are presenting your offering at the altar, and while there you remember that your brother has something [such as a grievance or legitimate complaint] against you,
AMP.24: leave your offering there at the altar and go. First make peace with your brother, and then come and present your offering.
AMP.25: Come to terms quickly [at the earliest opportunity] with your opponent at law while you are with him on the way [to court], so that your opponent does not hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you are thrown into prison.
AMP.26: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, you will not come out of there until you have paid the last cent.
AMP.27: "You have heard that it was said, '<e>You shall not commit adultery</e>';
AMP.28: but I say to you that everyone who [so much as] looks at a woman with lust for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
AMP.29: If your right eye makes you stumble and leads you to sin, tear it out and throw it away [that is, remove yourself from the source of temptation]; for it is better for you to lose one of the parts of your body, than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
AMP.30: If your right hand makes you stumble and leads you to sin, cut it off and throw it away [that is, remove yourself from the source of temptation]; for it is better for you to lose one of the parts of your body than for your whole body to go into hell.
AMP.31: "It has also been said, '<e>Whoever divorces his wife is to give her a certificate of divorce</e>';
AMP.32: but I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except on grounds of sexual immorality, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a woman who has been divorced commits adultery.
AMP.33: "Again, you have heard that it was said to the men of old, 'You shall not make false vows, but you shall fulfill your vows to the Lord [as a religious duty].'
AMP.34: But I say to you, do not make an oath at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God;
AMP.35: or by the earth, for it is the footstool of His feet; or by Jerusalem, for it is <e>the city of the Great King</e>.
AMP.36: Nor shall you make an oath by your head, for you are not able to make a single hair white or black.
AMP.37: But let your statement be, 'Yes, yes' or 'No, no' [a firm yes or no]; anything more than that comes from the evil one.
AMP.38: "You have heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth [punishment that fits the offense].'
AMP.39: But I say to you, do not resist an evil person [who insults you or violates your rights]; but whoever slaps you on the right cheek, turn the other toward him also [simply ignore insignificant insults or trivial losses and do not bother to retaliate—maintain your dignity, your self-respect, your poise].
AMP.40: If anyone wants to sue you and take your shirt, let him have your coat also [for the Lord repays the offender].
AMP.41: And whoever forces you to go one mile, go with him two.
AMP.42: Give to him who asks of you, and do not turn away from him who wants to borrow from you.
AMP.43: "You have heard that it was said, '<e>You shall love your neighbor</e> (fellow man) and hate your enemy.'
AMP.44: "But I say to you, love [that is, unselfishly seek the best or higher good for] your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
AMP.45: so that you may [show yourselves to] be the children of your Father who is in heaven; for He makes His sun rise on those who are evil and on those who are good, and makes the rain fall on the righteous [those who are morally upright] and the unrighteous [the unrepentant, those who oppose Him].
AMP.46: For if you love [only] those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do that?
AMP.47: And if you greet only your brothers [wishing them God's blessing and peace], what more [than others] are you doing? Do not even the Gentiles [who do not know the Lord] do that?
AMP.48: You, therefore, will be perfect [growing into spiritual maturity both in mind and character, actively integrating godly values into your daily life], as your heavenly Father is perfect.

MSG.1: When Jesus saw his ministry drawing huge crowds, he climbed a hillside. Those who were apprenticed to him, the committed, climbed with him. Arriving at a quiet place, he sat down
MSG.2: and taught his climbing companions. This is what he said:
MSG.3: "You're blessed when you're at the end of your rope. With less of you there is more of God and his rule.
MSG.4: "You're blessed when you feel you've lost what is most dear to you. Only then can you be embraced by the One most dear to you.
MSG.5: "You're blessed when you're content with just who you are—no more, no less. That's the moment you find yourselves proud owners of everything that can't be bought.
MSG.6: "You're blessed when you've worked up a good appetite for God. He's food and drink in the best meal you'll ever eat.
MSG.7: "You're blessed when you care. At the moment of being 'carefull,' you find yourselves cared for.
MSG.8: "You're blessed when you get your inside world—your mind and heart—put right. Then you can see God in the outside world.
MSG.9: "You're blessed when you can show people how to cooperate instead of compete or fight. That's when you discover who you really are, and your place in God's family.
MSG.10: "You're blessed when your commitment to God provokes persecution. The persecution drives you even deeper into God's kingdom.
MSG.11: "Not only that—count yourselves blessed every time people put you down or throw you out or speak lies about you to discredit me. What it means is that the truth is too close for comfort and they are uncomfortable.
MSG.12: You can be glad when that happens—give a cheer, even!—for though they don't like it, I do! And all heaven applauds. And know that you are in good company. My prophets and witnesses have always gotten into this kind of trouble.
MSG.13: "Let me tell you why you are here. You're here to be salt-seasoning that brings out the God-flavors of this earth. If you lose your saltiness, how will people taste godliness? You've lost your usefulness and will end up in the garbage.
MSG.14: "Here's another way to put it: You're here to be light, bringing out the God-colors in the world. God is not a secret to be kept. We're going public with this, as public as a city on a hill.
MSG.15: If I make you light-bearers, you don't think I'm going to hide you under a bucket, do you? I'm putting you on a light stand.
MSG.16: Now that I've put you there on a hilltop, on a light stand—shine! Keep open house; be generous with your lives. By opening up to others, you'll prompt people to open up with God, this generous Father in heaven.
MSG.17: "Don't suppose for a minute that I have come to demolish the Scriptures—either God's Law or the Prophets. I'm not here to demolish but to complete. I am going to put it all together, pull it all together in a vast panorama.
MSG.18: God's Law is more real and lasting than the stars in the sky and the ground at your feet. Long after stars burn out and earth wears out, God's Law will be alive and working.
MSG.19: "Trivialize even the smallest item in God's Law and you will only have trivialized yourself. But take it seriously, show the way for others, and you will find honor in the kingdom.
MSG.20: Unless you do far better than the Pharisees in the matters of right living, you won't know the first thing about entering the kingdom.
MSG.21: "You're familiar with the command to the ancients, 'Do not murder.'
MSG.22: I'm telling you that anyone who is so much as angry with a brother or sister is guilty of murder. Carelessly call a brother 'idiot!' and you just might find yourself hauled into court. Thoughtlessly yell 'stupid!' at a sister and you are on the brink of hellfire. The simple moral fact is that words kill.
MSG.23: "This is how I want you to conduct yourself in these matters. If you enter your place of worship and, about to make an offering, you suddenly remember a grudge a friend has against you,
MSG.24: abandon your offering, leave immediately, go to this friend and make things right. Then and only then, come back and work things out with God.
MSG.25: "Or say you're out on the street and an old enemy accosts you. Don't lose a minute. Make the first move; make things right with him. After all, if you leave the first move to him, knowing his track record, you're likely to end up in court, maybe even jail.
MSG.26: If that happens, you won't get out without a stiff fine.
MSG.27: "You know the next commandment pretty well, too: 'Don't go to bed with another's spouse.'
MSG.28: But don't think you've preserved your virtue simply by staying out of bed. Your heart can be corrupted by lust even quicker than your body. Those leering looks you think nobody notices—they also corrupt.
MSG.29: "Let's not pretend this is easier than it really is. If you want to live a morally pure life, here's what you have to do: You have to blind your right eye the moment you catch it in a lustful leer. You have to choose to live one-eyed or else be dumped on a moral trash pile.
MSG.30: And you have to chop off your right hand the moment you notice it raised threateningly. Better a bloody stump than your entire being discarded for good in the dump.
MSG.31: "Remember the Scripture that says, 'Whoever divorces his wife, let him do it legally, giving her divorce papers and her legal rights'?
MSG.32: Too many of you are using that as a cover for selfishness and whim, pretending to be righteous just because you are 'legal.' Please, no more pretending. If you divorce your wife, you're responsible for making her an adulteress (unless she has already made herself that by sexual promiscuity). And if you marry such a divorced adulteress, you're automatically an adulterer yourself. You can't use legal cover to mask a moral failure.
MSG.33: "And don't say anything you don't mean. This counsel is embedded deep in our traditions.
MSG.34: You only make things worse when you lay down a smoke screen of pious talk, saying, 'I'll pray for you,' and never doing it, or saying, 'God be with you,' and not meaning it. You don't make your words true by embellishing them with religious lace. In making your speech sound more religious, it becomes less true.
MSG.37: Just say 'yes' and 'no.' When you manipulate words to get your own way, you go wrong.
MSG.38: "Here's another old saying that deserves a second look: 'Eye for eye, tooth for tooth.'
MSG.39: Is that going to get us anywhere? Here's what I propose: 'Don't hit back at all.' If someone strikes you, stand there and take it.
MSG.40: If someone drags you into court and sues for the shirt off your back, giftwrap your best coat and make a present of it.
MSG.41: And if someone takes unfair advantage of you, use the occasion to practice the servant life.
MSG.42: No more tit-for-tat stuff. Live generously.
MSG.43: "You're familiar with the old written law, 'Love your friend,' and its unwritten companion, 'Hate your enemy.'
MSG.44: I'm challenging that. I'm telling you to love your enemies. Let them bring out the best in you, not the worst. When someone gives you a hard time, respond with the energies of prayer,
MSG.45: for then you are working out of your true selves, your God-created selves. This is what God does. He gives his best—the sun to warm and the rain to nourish—to everyone, regardless: the good and bad, the nice and nasty.
MSG.46: If all you do is love the lovable, do you expect a bonus? Anybody can do that.
MSG.47: If you simply say hello to those who greet you, do you expect a medal? Any run-of-the-mill sinner does that.
MSG.48: "In a word, what I'm saying is, Grow up. You're kingdom subjects. Now live like it. Live out your God-created identity. Live generously and graciously toward others, the way God lives toward you.

NIV.1:
The Beatitudes
Now when he saw the crowds, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came to him,
NIV.2: and he began to teach them, saying:
NIV.3:
“Blessed are the poor in spirit,
for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.4:
Blessed are those who mourn,
for they will be comforted.
NIV.5:
Blessed are the meek,
for they will inherit the earth.
NIV.6:
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness,
for they will be filled.
NIV.7:
Blessed are the merciful,
for they will be shown mercy.
NIV.8:
Blessed are the pure in heart,
for they will see God.
NIV.9:
Blessed are the peacemakers,
for they will be called sons of God.
NIV.10:
Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness,
for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.11: “Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of me.
NIV.12: Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
NIV.13:
Salt and Light
“You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled by men.
NIV.14: “You are the light of the world. A city on a hill cannot be hidden.
NIV.15: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.
NIV.16: In the same way, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good deeds and praise your Father in heaven.
NIV.17:
The Fulfillment of the Law
“Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.
NIV.18: I tell you the truth, until heaven and earth disappear, not the smallest letter, not the least stroke of a pen, will by any means disappear from the Law until everything is accomplished.
NIV.19: Anyone who breaks one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever practices and teaches these commands will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.20: For I tell you that unless your righteousness surpasses that of the Pharisees and the teachers of the law, you will certainly not enter the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.21:
Murder
“You have heard that it was said to the people long ago, ‘Do not murder, and anyone who murders will be subject to judgment.’
NIV.22: But I tell you that anyone who is angry with his brother will be subject to judgment. Again, anyone who says to his brother, ‘Raca,’ is answerable to the Sanhedrin. But anyone who says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of hell.
NIV.23: “Therefore, if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
NIV.24: leave your gift there in front of the altar. First go and be reconciled to your brother; then come and offer your gift.
NIV.25: “Settle matters quickly with your adversary who is taking you to court. Do it while you are still with him on the way, or he may hand you over to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.
NIV.26: I tell you the truth, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.
NIV.27:
Adultery
“You have heard that it was said, ‘Do not commit adultery.’
NIV.28: But I tell you that anyone who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NIV.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
NIV.30: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to go into hell.
NIV.31:
Divorce
“It has been said, ‘Anyone who divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.’
NIV.32: But I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for marital unfaithfulness, causes her to become an adulteress, and anyone who marries the divorced woman commits adultery.
NIV.33:
Oaths
“Again, you have heard that it was said to the people long ago, ‘Do not break your oath, but keep the oaths you have made to the Lord.’
NIV.34: But I tell you, Do not swear at all: either by heaven, for it is God’s throne;
NIV.35: or by the earth, for it is his footstool; or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the Great King.
NIV.36: And do not swear by your head, for you cannot make even one hair white or black.
NIV.37: Simply let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes,’ and your ‘No,’ ‘No’; anything beyond this comes from the evil one.
NIV.38:
An Eye for an Eye
“You have heard that it was said, ‘Eye for eye, and tooth for tooth.’
NIV.39: But I tell you, Do not resist an evil person. If someone strikes you on the right cheek, turn to him the other also.
NIV.40: And if someone wants to sue you and take your tunic, let him have your cloak as well.
NIV.41: If someone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
NIV.42: Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.
NIV.43:
Love for Enemies
“You have heard that it was said, ‘Love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
NIV.44: But I tell you: Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
NIV.45: that you may be sons of your Father in heaven. He causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
NIV.46: If you love those who love you, what reward will you get? Are not even the tax collectors doing that?
NIV.47: And if you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Do not even pagans do that?
NIV.48: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

NRSVCE.16: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father in heaven.
NRSVCE.17: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the law or the prophets; I have come not to abolish but to fulfill.
NRSVCE.18: For truly I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not one letter, not one stroke of a letter, will pass from the law until all is accomplished.
NRSVCE.19: Therefore, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others to do the same, will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
NRSVCE.20: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
NRSVCE.21: “You have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not murder’; and ‘whoever murders shall be liable to judgment.’
NRSVCE.22: But I say to you that if you are angry with a brother or sister, you will be liable to judgment; and if you insult a brother or sister, you will be liable to the council; and if you say, ‘You fool,’ you will be liable to the hell of fire.
NRSVCE.23: So when you are offering your gift at the altar, if you remember that your brother or sister has something against you,
NRSVCE.24: leave your gift there before the altar and go; first be reconciled to your brother or sister, and then come and offer your gift.
NRSVCE.25: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are on the way to court with him, or your accuser may hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you will be thrown into prison.
NRSVCE.26: Truly I tell you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
NRSVCE.27: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
NRSVCE.28: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NRSVCE.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
NRSVCE.30: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to go into hell.
NRSVCE.31: “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
NRSVCE.32: But I say to you that anyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of unchastity, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
NRSVCE.33: “Again, you have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not swear falsely, but carry out the vows you have made to the Lord.’
NRSVCE.34: But I say to you, Do not swear at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
NRSVCE.35: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
NRSVCE.36: And do not swear by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
NRSVCE.37: Let your word be ‘Yes, Yes’ or ‘No, No’; anything more than this comes from the evil one.
NRSVCE.38: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
NRSVCE.39: But I say to you, Do not resist an evildoer. But if anyone strikes you on the right cheek, turn the other also;
NRSVCE.40: and if anyone wants to sue you and take your coat, give your cloak as well;
NRSVCE.41: and if anyone forces you to go one mile, go also the second mile.
NRSVCE.42: Give to everyone who begs from you, and do not refuse anyone who wants to borrow from you.
NRSVCE.43: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
NRSVCE.44: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
NRSVCE.45: so that you may be children of your Father in heaven; for he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the righteous and on the unrighteous.
NRSVCE.46: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
NRSVCE.47: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
NRSVCE.48: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

NET.1: When he saw the crowds, he went up the mountain. After he sat down his disciples came to him.
NET.2: Then he began to teach them by saying:
NET.3: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
NET.4: “Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
NET.5: “Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
NET.6: “Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be satisfied.
NET.7: “Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.
NET.8: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
NET.9: “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called the children of God.
NET.10: “Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
NET.11: “Blessed are you when people insult you and persecute you and say all kinds of evil things about you falsely on account of me.
NET.12: Rejoice and be glad because your reward is great in heaven, for they persecuted the prophets before you in the same way.
NET.13: “You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its flavor, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything except to be thrown out and trampled on by people.
NET.14: You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill cannot be hidden.
NET.15: People do not light a lamp and put it under a basket but on a lampstand, and it gives light to all in the house.
NET.16: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so that they can see your good deeds and give honor to your Father in heaven.
NET.17: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the law or the prophets. I have not come to abolish these things but to fulfill them.
NET.18: I tell you the truth, until heaven and earth pass away not the smallest letter or stroke of a letter will pass from the law until everything takes place.
NET.19: So anyone who breaks one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do so will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever obeys them and teaches others to do so will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
NET.20: For I tell you, unless your righteousness goes beyond that of the experts in the law and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
NET.21: “You have heard that it was said to an older generation, ‘Do not murder,’ and ‘whoever murders will be subjected to judgment.’
NET.22: But I say to you that anyone who is angry with a brother will be subjected to judgment. And whoever insults a brother will be brought before the council, and whoever says ‘Fool’ will be sent to fiery hell.
NET.23: So then, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
NET.24: leave your gift there in front of the altar. First go and be reconciled to your brother and then come and present your gift.
NET.25: Reach agreement quickly with your accuser while on the way to court, or he may hand you over to the judge, and the judge hand you over to the warden, and you will be thrown into prison.
NET.26: I tell you the truth, you will never get out of there until you have paid the last penny!
NET.27: “You have heard that it was said, ‘Do not commit adultery.’
NET.28: But I say to you that whoever looks at a woman to desire her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
NET.29: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away! It is better to lose one of your members than to have your whole body thrown into hell.
NET.30: If your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away! It is better to lose one of your members than to have your whole body go into hell.
NET.31: “It was said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife must give her a legal document.’
NET.32: But I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except for immorality, makes her commit adultery, and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
NET.33: “Again, you have heard that it was said to an older generation, ‘Do not break an oath, but fulfill your vows to the Lord.’
NET.34: But I say to you, do not take oaths at all — not by heaven, because it is the throne of God,
NET.35: not by earth, because it is his footstool, and not by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.
NET.36: Do not take an oath by your head, because you are not able to make one hair white or black.
NET.37: Let your word be ‘Yes, yes’ or ‘No, no.’ More than this is from the evil one.
NET.38: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
NET.39: But I say to you, do not resist the evildoer. But whoever strikes you on the right cheek, turn the other to him as well.
NET.40: And if someone wants to sue you and to take your tunic, give him your coat also.
NET.41: And if anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two.
NET.42: Give to the one who asks you, and do not reject the one who wants to borrow from you.
NET.43: “You have heard that it was said, ‘Love your neighbor’ and ‘hate your enemy.’
NET.44: But I say to you, love your enemy and pray for those who persecute you,
NET.45: so that you may be like your Father in heaven, since he causes the sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
NET.46: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Even the tax collectors do the same, don’t they?
NET.47: And if you only greet your brothers, what more do you do? Even the Gentiles do the same, don’t they?
NET.48: So then, be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
